raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that the optimal treatment plan for the seven-year-old child with deep carious lesions is represented by option B – a conservative approach such as indirect pulp capping followed by appropriate cavity restoration. However, I want to add another important point regarding the necessity of ensuring thorough disinfection before any restorative procedure. Ensuring the lesion site is free of bacteria will significantly increase the success rate of the restoration and reduce the risk of recurrent infection or need for further intervention down the line.

Thus, my answer remains **B**, aligned with minimizing surgical invasiveness while setting up conditions conducive to successful healing and long-lasting results. Answer choice: B.
